title: audit — retention windows
description: Fixed regulatory windows. NEVER hard-delete audit rows — anonymise-not-delete is the contract.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5 (storage limitation) + Art. 17 (erasure via anonymisation)

purge.trigger: created_at < now() - 365d AND cold_key IS NULL
purge.action: rotate to cold: KMS-envelope-encrypt + S3 Glacier + Object Lock immutable retention. Row updated with cold_key; old_values/new_values NULLed in Postgres. row_hash + previous_hash retained.

cold.description: 7 years in S3 Glacier with Object Lock. Non-mutable during window.

purge.action: anonymise-not-delete: replace workspace_id / user_id / auditable_id with anonymised placeholders. row_hash retained for chain continuity. cold_key retained but S3 object may be lifecycle-expired.
anonymise workspace_id to hash + retain rows for cross-workspace compliance evidence
anonymise user_id / user_type / PII in old_values / new_values — row retained for workspace audit continuity

chain_integrity.note: row_hash + previous_hash NEVER anonymised. Chain must remain verifiable regardless of PII anonymisation. Post-anonymisation, hash still validates against canonical serialisation because PII fields are replaced with deterministic anonymised placeholders (hash of original).
